level_signals: 6+ years of product management or related industry roles
Expected to define strategic priorities and product roadmaps
Leads cross-functional collaboration with research, engineering, policy

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have 6+ years of product management or related industry roles, with specific expertise in AI safety, trust & safety, integrity, or related domains.
must_haves.qualifier: with specific expertise in AI safety, trust & safety, integrity, or related domains

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: This role suits a proactive, technically skilled product manager adept at adversarial thinking and excited to tackle challenging, ambiguous problems through structured analysis and collaborative decision-making.
Have hands-on experience driving consensus and action in ambiguous spaces.
Excel at identifying and challenging underlying assumptions and constraints through insightful questioning.
Are highly effective at cross-functional collaboration and communicating complex technical concepts clearly and persuasively.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Are deeply curious and interested in interdisciplinary fields such as human-computer interaction, psychology, philosophy, or similar areas.
Enjoy working in a fast-paced, high-growth environment.

scope.surface: Biosecurity risk safety systems for OpenAI's frontier model deployments
scope.business_model: AI research and deployment company, B2C and B2B
primary_metrics_inferred: Reliability and effectiveness of deployed safety systems
core_responsibilities: Partner closely with research, engineering, data science, policy teams, and other stakeholders to embed safety throughout the development and deployment of frontier AI models
Develop comprehensive frameworks for understanding and mitigating deployment safety risks, drawing on data analysis, expert consultation, and adversarial assessments
Define strategic priorities and product roadmaps focused on improving deployment safety, enhancing reliability, and managing emerging AI capabilities
Create scalable methodologies, tools, and processes for evaluating, refining, and continuously improving our safety systems
Establish repeatable processes to integrate cutting-edge AI safety research into OpenAI's models and product offerings
Develop and continuously refine clear, actionable metrics that effectively capture safety performance and user experience at scale
Create a plan to uplift the ecosystem, accelerating others to outpace adversaries

green_flags: Highly specific and mission-critical role with clear ownership area (biosecurity risk)
Cross-functional visibility spanning research, engineering, data science, and policy
OpenAI is a top-tier AI lab with significant resources and impact
Clear description of responsibilities and what success looks like
red_flags: Onsite-only in San Francisco — no remote or hybrid option stated
Biology background listed as a 'you might thrive' bullet but is functionally essential for a biosecurity-focused PM role — ambiguous whether it is truly optional
High ambiguity in role scope: 'adversarial thinking' and 'ecosystem uplift' are vague without clearer deliverables

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 6+ years requirement and responsibility scope)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed in JD)
location.type (inferred as onsite from 'This position is based in San Francisco, CA'; no hybrid or remote language present)
ambiguities: 'Have a background in biology' appears under 'You might thrive in this role if you:' alongside other items framed as conditional, but for a biosecurity-focused PM role this is likely a hard requirement. Classified as hard severity; flagged here for review.
Visa sponsorship is not mentioned anywhere in the JD. Work auth treated as open but sponsorship classified as unclear.

The 'You might thrive in this role if you:' section is used for all requirements — both likely-hard (6+ years PM, biology background) and likely-soft (curiosity, fast-paced preference). Severity classifications made based on specificity and domain relevance.
